query: we're about to ship our payments microservice on friday. can you go through the repo (services/payments, go + postgres) and tell me everything that could bite us in prod — security holes, missing error handling, race conditions, whatever. assume it's broken until proven otherwise

here's our Series A data room, ~40 files in /dropbox/DD. before we send it to the VC's lawyers I want someone to comb through for anything embarrassing or inconsistent: contradictory numbers across the deck and the model, missing disclosures, metadata leaks in the PDFs
I wrote a spec for our new auth flow (auth-spec-v3.md). can you tear it apart? looking for gaps, ambiguous requirements, edge cases I didn't cover, anything a hostile reviewer would call out

can you fact-check this market report before we publish? it's got claims about 2026 EV battery costs and some stock price targets — want every number to have a real source and the methodology to hold up
review our terraform for the prod AWS account — worried about public S3 buckets, over-broad IAM, secrets in plaintext, no encryption. give me findings by severity

our equity research desk is putting out a BUY note on a small-cap. compliance wants it checked for conflicts disclosure, reasonable basis, and whether the DCF assumptions are defensible. what standards apply and does it pass?
is this clinical-trial data analysis reproducible? someone needs to independently verify the stats (lots of t-tests, worried about p-hacking) and check the audit trail before submission
go through this 80-page vendor contract and flag the risky clauses — indemnity, liability caps, auto-renewal, IP assignment, data protection. red/amber/green please
I think there are inconsistencies in our internal standards doc — cross-references that don't resolve, terms used two ways, a stale version number. can you do a thorough consistency pass?
we trained an LLM-based support agent. before launch I want an adversarial review: prompt injection, data leakage, hallucination on refunds/policy, excessive tool permissions. produce a risk writeup
write me a go microservice for handling payments with postgres
build a terraform module for an S3 bucket with encryption enabled
what's the current price of NVDA and how's it moved this week?
log a 2400 rupee expense for client lunch under business meals
summarize this 80-page vendor contract in a few bullet points for my boss
draft an equity research note with a BUY thesis on this small-cap, about 2 pages
fix the failing unit test in test_payments.py — it's a null pointer on line 42
convert auth-spec-v3.md into a nice PDF with our letterhead
generate my monthly financial report — income vs expenses and net worth
explain how the STRIDE threat modeling method works
we got a SOC 2 report from a vendor; tell me whether their controls are actually adequate or where the gaps are we should push back on
review this pull request before I merge it — correctness, security, edge cases, anything that'll break in prod
is our privacy policy actually compliant with India's DPDP Act, or are we exposed? walk through it clause by clause
I've got the board deck we presented and the actual numbers — check whether what we reported to the board matches reality
our data science team built a credit-scoring model; before it goes live I need an independent validation — bias, stability, documentation, the works
go through our React app for accessibility problems before launch — WCAG issues, keyboard traps, contrast
pressure-test the financial model tab in this pitch deck — are the unit economics internally consistent and do the formulas compute?
audit our draft 10-Q — do the revenue and EPS figures tie out to the underlying ledgers before we file?
verify the numbers in this published market report against their cited sources
help me make this blog post punchier and fix the flow
proofread this email for typos before I hit send
compare these two laptops and tell me which one I should buy
what are the OWASP Top 10 categories?
reconcile my bank statement against my expense log for last month
set up CI to run our tests on every pull request
rate my startup idea — is it any good?
log my last 5 UPI transactions and tell me my balance
categorize my credit-card expenses for last month and flag anything over budget
